rag_rules: Keep each annex block with the owner scope it was generated for: type-level contracts stay on component types, and implementation-level content stays on implementations.
Use the extracted AADL structure as the authority for valid owners and scopes before inserting any annex content.
Preserve the existing architecture during fusion: do not alter component names, features, subcomponents, connections, or property associations unless the edit explicitly targets annex content.
generic_rules: Insert new annex content as a scoped addition, not as a structural rewrite of the surrounding AADL declaration.
If an owner already has annex content, merge carefully without disturbing unrelated declarations or changing their order unnecessarily.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AADL annex blocks use the form: annex agree {** ... **};
Implementation-scoped content must remain after the declarations it depends on, while type-scoped content must remain within the component type declaration.
anti_patterns: Do not move content between a component type and its implementation just to make insertion easier.
Do not introduce references to subcomponent internals into a type-level scope or otherwise broaden visibility during fusion.
irrelevant_topics: Rules about numeric comparisons on Boolean or opaque data
Guidance about using AADL property names as expression variables
